name: Remove K Digits
category: Stack and Queues [Learning, Pre-In-Post-fix, Monotonic Stack, Implementation]
statement:
Given a string nums representing a non-negative integer, and an integer k, find the smallest possible integer after removing k digits from num.


Note: If removing k digits deletes all digits, return "0". The result must be a valid non-negative integer without leading zeros.
constraints:
•   1 <= k <= nums.length <= 104
  •   nums consists of only digits.
  •   nums does not have any leading zeros except for the zero itself.

examples.explanation: Removing the two digits 5 and 4 yields the smallest number, 1892.
Remove the three digits 1(leading one), 9, and 9 to form the new number 21(Note that the output must not contain leading zeroes) which is the smallest.
